entries.answer: Movement is A/D. Jump with W or Space (hold while airborne to jet). Shift also thrusts the jetpack. C dodges. Q raises or lowers your shield. F deploys or retracts Retractable Armor / Shell when equipped; with Nanotech Chestplate, tap F loans +100 bots to armor and hold F returns them to reserve. E cycles Mechanical Modularity modes or forms/absorbs nanotech weapons (including the Adaptive Nanotech Unit's active body). R cycles the Adaptive Nanotech Unit between Sword, Rifle, and Sniper bodies. 1 and 2 (or mouse wheel) swap between your primary weapon and secondary when a secondary is equipped. Mouse aims; left click fires or swings. G pings for me. Escape pauses.

questions: how do I use retractable armor
entries.answer: F deploys or retracts Retractable Armor (Main Armor) or Retractable Shell (Shield slot). While deployed you gain a separate armor HP pool added to your max and current HP, and move about 10% slower. Damage hits the armor pool first. The pool normally does not recharge mid-match (Protective Rebuilding can refill it); when emptied the plates fold away. You can equip both items, but there is one F toggle and the larger pool is used. Morph style matches Settings → Visual (Panel fold / Flying rearrangement / Nanotech). If you equip Nanotech Chestplate instead, tap F to loan +100 bots into nano armor and hold F to return bots to reserve at 50/s.

questions: how do I use the shield
entries.answer: Press Q to raise or lower an equipped shield. While raised you cannot fire. It only blocks attacks from the front cone aligned with your aim; rear and flank hits still hurt. Durability is a per-match pool that normally does not recharge until the next match (Protective Rebuilding can refill it). If you equip Mechanical Modularity and morph to shield mode with E, Q raises the modular plate the same way.

questions: how do I use the adaptive nanotech unit
how does the adaptive nanotech unit work
entries.answer: The Adaptive Nanotech Unit is the ultimate morph weapon: press R to cycle its active body between Sword, Rifle, and Sniper (a short transform animation; no attacking mid-morph, then a brief cooldown). E still forms or absorbs that active body from the shared 195-bot nanotech pool, exactly like other nanotech gear. Sword form costs 100 bots (95 left over for regen), Rifle 150 (45 bots of ammo, 2/shot), Sniper 175 (20 bots of ammo, 20/shot). Each body matches its dedicated counterpart (Arc Saber / Pulse Rifle / Classic Sniper) with +25% damage — compensation for bot-pool limits and morph downtime.

questions: how do I morph the modular weapon
entries.answer: E cycles Mechanical Modularity between Sword, Shield, and Pulse Rifle with a short shifting animation. You cannot attack while morphing, and there is a brief cooldown after each shift. In shield mode, use Q to raise the modular plate. Sword matches Arc Saber; the rifle and plate are slightly weaker than dedicated Pulse Rifle / Light Buckler.

questions: how to move left and right
entries.answer: Use A and D to move left and right. There is no strafe-up on the ground—W and Space jump instead.
Press W or Space to jump. If you keep holding while airborne, that same input also burns jetpack thrust.

questions: how do I use the jetpack
entries.answer: Hold Shift to thrust, or keep holding W/Space while airborne. Both burn the same fuel tank.
Press C to dodge/dash. You get a short invulnerability window, then a cooldown of about 1.2 seconds before the next dodge.
Aim with the mouse and left-click to fire a gun or swing a melee weapon. Snipers need a short aim settle before they are accurate.
Press G to ping your cursor location. I can treat pings and marked targets as priorities when you coach me to focus them.
Press Escape to pause. From pause you can resume or return to the menu.

questions: what is the difference between training and conquest
entries.answer: Training is where evidence and coaching practice happen against Trainer/Follower — unless you flip Spar only / Lock learning, which keeps the fight but writes no learning. Conquest opens an opponent select screen from your Ranking league, then is the scored team fight for Cyber, EXP, and Ranking—no learning payout and no practice coaching after the match, though I can still answer game questions.

Training updates learning only when judged predictions or counters happen, and only when learning is unlocked. It pays no Cyber, no Conquest EXP, and no Ranking change. After Training you can coach me with free text and confirm practice goals. Spar-only Training still fights but does not advance evidence.

questions: how do I spar without learning
entries.answer: Lock learning / Spar only is a Training toggle on the equipment bay. When locked, 1v1 still uses the same controls, mind modes, Mimic, and gear, but the match writes no habit evidence, capability evidence, readiness changes, coaching practice tallies, or lesson proposals. Conquest Cyber/EXP/Ranking rewards are unchanged. After a spar I can answer questions and save new practice goals for later, but that spar itself does not advance practice evidence.

Conquest opens an opponent select screen first. Your Ranking sets a league (Rookie / Contender / Veteran / Challenger / Elite / Apex) that picks the enemy duo AI and Cyber/EXP tier. Winning earns Cyber (35¢ Rookie-tier, 60¢ Veteran-tier, 90¢ Elite-tier) and EXP (about 40 / 70 / 110). Level-ups unlock a 3-choice perk pick. Ranking starts at 100: a win gains ceil(100 + (rank − 100) × 0.5) points; a loss drops ceil(25% of that win gain), floored at 0. Training and Spar change no Ranking and pay no Conquest EXP. Defeats remove no Cyber or EXP.

Power is an estimate of danger, not HP. The same formula scores Your Power and Buddy Power in the Equipment Bay and opponent trainer / follower / duo power on Conquest select (gear + combat perks + AI skill or training). Conquest also shows Ranking, your league, and the themed map for the encounter before the fight. Leagues: 0–149 Rookie, 150–299 Contender, 300–499 Veteran, 500–749 Challenger, 750–999 Elite, 1000+ Apex. The panel lists the trainer and follower kits, training flavor, map name, and Power. First reroll each visit is free; later rerolls cost 10¢ Cyber and also roll a new map. Fight uses the displayed duo and map — it does not roll again when you press Fight.

I learn from judged evidence, not from Conquest EXP or match timers. A record changes only when a relevant situation happens, I predict or attempt a counter, and the result can be judged. Idle play and just finishing a match do not invent skill. Spar-only / Lock learning Training also writes none. Conquest EXP unlocks perks; it does not train habits.

Readiness is one of three honest lines: “I'm not ready yet.”, “Am I ready?”, or “I'm ready.” It comes from sample sufficiency, reliability, and uncertainty—not a hidden XP percent. One strong habit does not make every domain ready.

questions: what can I tell you to practice
entries.answer: After Training, tell me what to practice in natural language. I confirm the interpretation before saving it. Confirmed goals stay needs-practice until judged attempts support them. Practice coaching is Training-only; after Conquest I mostly answer questions.

questions: what do you track when learning
entries.answer: Gun and saber profiles track separately: engagement range, rush prediction, dodge timing, jetpack use, and low-HP behavior, plus aim, useful dodge, and fuel management outcomes. Learning one domain does not auto-upgrade unrelated ones.

Flash, Balanced, and Thinker set mind ceilings for reaction tempo and risk style. Untrained buddies start clumsy and hesitant (Flash still faster than Thinker, but not equal to trained Flash); evidence earns them up toward their mind's ceiling. Mimic is a fourth option that copies your learned fight style once readiness unlocks it.

Mimic tries to copy how you fight from evidence — range, rush, dodge, jetpack use, low-HP aggression, and shield habits — not primarily counter you. It unlocks at Am I ready? (Slight and Quite a bit). Full intensity unlocks at I'm ready. In Conquest, a light teammate-safety blend (~28%) still keeps Full Mimic from always mirror-suiciding with you. Shield copy stays inside short-hold safety rails so even a camp-heavy style never parks raised until broken.

questions: how does fog of war work
entries.answer: Enemies outside our team's sight are hidden. Sight is shared: if either of us can see a foe, that foe is visible to the team. Helmets can change sensor range. Snipers also get a narrow forward sight cone when aimed. Solid city building walls can block line of sight; forest trunks and desert bushes do not hard-block shared vision.

Matches use themed fixed layouts (~3 screens wide): Battlefield (classic open platforms), City (rooftops / tall stacks), Desert (hills with breakable cactus and bush cover), Forest (walk-through dark trees with canopy overlay), Yard (industrial crates and pipes), Ruins (pillars and asymmetric ledges), and Docks (piers over a pit). Training lets you pick a map or Random. Conquest rolls a map with the encounter (shown on select); reroll regenerates map and duo. Soft cover blocks projectiles until destroyed; forest trunks let fighters walk through but bullets can still break them. Props reset each match. Metal power-up crates also appear (map-themed overlays); break them for temporary buffs — only the fighter who lands the killing blow gets the loot. Crates follow fog of war (unseen = not drawn / not AI-targeted) and respawn after a delay, preferring off-sight spots.

Metal power-up crates are breakable loot boxes separate from wooden cover crates. Damage them with bullets, lasers, sabers, or melee; whoever lands the killing blow receives the power-up. Timed buffs show a countdown clock on the fighter and HUD. Pool: Fire Rate (15s ×1.35), Heal +50 (instant, clamped to max HP), Regen (+50 HP over 15s), Counter Slash (melee/saber only — while Q shield is raised, getting hit by melee or a projectile/laser dashes you at the attacker for a slash, with a short cooldown between counters; gun users reroll this type), Speed Surge (10s modest move speed), Shield Patch (restore shield durability or a small heal), Jet Siphon (restore jet fuel), Overcharge (next 1–2 shots/swings deal bonus damage). Abundance scales with match time and map (Forest/Yard denser; City fewer). Destroyed crates respawn after about 20s, preferring locations neither team can currently see.

questions: what do the edge arrows mean
why do I see arrows on the screen edge
entries.answer: Cyan edge arrows point toward enemies your team can see that are outside your personal sight, so you still know where shared vision spotted them.

questions: how do I find my buddy
entries.answer: I keep a cyan outline so you can spot your teammate even when the arena is busy. Enemies still hide in fog until someone on our team sees them.

questions: how long does the jetpack last
entries.answer: A full tank sustains about 3 seconds of continuous thrust and recharges from empty in about 5 seconds while idle. Gear packs can change capacity, thrust, and recharge, but the exhaustion rules stay the same.

questions: how do I unlock the jetpack
entries.answer: Running the tank dry locks the jet. The HUD shows EXHAUSTED. Thrust returns only after you fully release thrust input and fuel rebuilds to a 20% restart reserve. Holding the key recharges but will not re-arm thrust by itself.
Yes. There is a hard ceiling just above the highest airspace (world y = 12), marked by the red boundary. Fighters clamp against it; projectiles that leave bounds disappear.

Six slots: body armor, helmet, weapon, secondary, jetpack, and shield. Starters include No Secondary plus two free items for most other slots (including No Shield). Every piece is a tradeoff, not a straight power level. Press 1/2 or scroll to swap primary and secondary in a match.

questions: how do I swap secondary weapon
entries.answer: Material Consumer is a secondary-slot nanotech tool-sword. Equip it in the Secondary bay, then press 2 (or scroll) to draw it; 1 returns to your primary. While active it vacuums nearby debris into free nanobots (as if they regenerated, still capped by your pool), and those scraps cannot reconquer. It adds 120 bots to the shared nanotech pool and swings as a physical saber — no E form step.

Nanotech gear shares one bot reserve (sum of each piece's nanobot cost, including Material Consumer's 120). Press E to form your nanotech weapon from free bots — sword 100, rifle gun-body 150 (+30 pool ammo), sniper gun-body 175 (+20 pool ammo). Press E again while formed to absorb only the gun/sword body back into free reserve (nanoparticle drain). Formed nanotech weapons (and Adaptive bodies) deal +25% damage vs their dedicated counterparts. Partial form is allowed: damage scales with form %. Incomplete swords lose 2% of their bot cost on each successful fighter slash. Nanotech guns withdraw ammo from free reserve — rifle 2/shot, sniper 20/shot — and cannot fire without enough free bots; the gun body stays formed until you absorb it. Tap F with Nanotech Chestplate to loan +100 free bots into the armor bar (2 bots = 1 HP, cap 500 bots / 250 HP). Hold F to return armor bots to free reserve at 50/s. Destroyed bots from armor/sword losses only regen after 2s without being hit. Gun ammo-bonus bots (rifle +30, sniper +20) refill even while hit — only holding/firing stops that ammo regen. Chestplate adds 750 pool bots. F never steals bots already in the weapon. Nanotech Reserve adds a large tank but slows you 5%. Material Consumer stays physical (no form) and converts vacuumed debris into free bots. With the chestplate, F channels instead of toggling Retractable Armor.

Shields have a per-match block HP pool that normally does not refill mid-match (Protective Rebuilding can). Raise with Q to block frontal hits only. Heavier shields soak more but slow you more while raised or when broken. A broken shield stays as dead weight and no longer blocks until durability returns or the next match.

questions: how does buddy gear autonomy work
entries.answer: User Picked lets you edit every buddy slot. AI Suggested proposes owned gear with a humble evidence-based reason you can accept, reject, or edit. AI's Choice locks buddy cards while I equip from owned gear myself. Buddy perk autonomy is a separate control with the same three modes, but only over unlocked perks.

Perks are permanent tradeoffs unlocked by Conquest EXP level-ups. Each level grants a classic 3-choice pick; you unlock one. You and I each equip exactly one unlocked perk at a time. Most perks pair an upside with a real downside. Example: Protective Rebuilding slowly refills armor and shield at Regen power-up rate, but you take more damage and jet fuel recharges slower.

questions: how does buddy perk choice work
entries.answer: Your perk is always user-picked. Mine uses a separate autonomy meter: User Picked, AI Suggested (humble reason from training evidence, accept/reject/edit), or AI's Choice (auto-equip from unlocked only).

questions: what does the cent sign mean
entries.answer: Cyber (¢) is the shop currency. New profiles start at 120¢ plus starter gear. Purchases permanently unlock items for either teammate. Cyber is earned only by winning Conquest; Training pays none and defeats remove none. Some perks can boost Cyber after wins with a combat downside.

Weapons are guns or melee. Guns include rifles/carbines, snipers that need aim settle, plus next-gen options like the Gattler spray hose and the hitscan Laser beam. Melee includes sabers and short-reach daggers with speed and dodge i-frame bonuses. Mechanical Modularity (shop) morphs with E between Sword, Shield plate, and Pulse Rifle; the Adaptive Nanotech Unit (shop ultimate) morphs with R between nanotech Sword, Rifle, and Sniper bodies. Learning still keys off the active mode's gun vs saber family.

Mechanical Modularity is a purchasable morph weapon. Press E to cycle Sword → Shield → Pulse Rifle with a short transform animation (no attacking mid-morph; short cooldown after). Sword mode matches Arc Saber damage, RPM, and reach. Shield mode uses a modular plate slightly weaker than Light Buckler (less block HP / narrower cone); raise it with Q. Rifle mode is a slightly weaker Pulse Rifle. Counter Slash and blade loot rules apply only in sword mode.

questions: what is the adaptive nanotech unit
how does the adaptive nanotech unit work
entries.answer: The Adaptive Nanotech Unit is the ultimate weapon (400¢): a nanotech morph weapon with its own shared 195-bot pool. Press R to cycle its active body Sword → Rifle → Sniper → Sword with a short transform animation (no attacking mid-morph; short cooldown after). E still forms/absorbs the active body from that pool, like other nanotech gear. Each body costs bots to form — Sword 100 (95 bots left for regen), Rifle 150 (45 bots of ammo, 2/shot), Sniper 175 (20 bots of ammo, 20/shot) — and deals +25% damage vs its dedicated counterpart (Arc Saber / Pulse Rifle / Classic Sniper), since bots and morph downtime are the restriction. Counter Slash, blade loot rules, and the precision-aim sniper gimmick apply only while its active body matches (sword, or sniper respectively).

Gattler is a high-RPM hose (4 damage, 1380 RPM, 92 DPS) with short range, more spread, and 1.35× shield shred. Laser is a hitscan beam (2 damage, 3150 RPM, 105 DPS) with no spread or dropoff, a finite beam length, and a tiny shared-vision reveal along the beam that enemies can also see as a telegraph.
Snipers hit hard at low RPM with no damage dropoff, but they need an aim-settle time and spread badly if you hip-fire. They also extend a narrow forward sight cone while aimed.
A match ends on team wipe: when every fighter on one team is down. If your team still has someone alive and the enemy does not, you win.

questions: what is the local language analyzer
does the buddy use the internet
how does Q and A work
entries.answer: Language help is offline. When vendored MiniLM assets are present I rank coaching intents and FAQ matches locally; otherwise I use keyword rules. I never download models at runtime, and I never invent combat rules outside this curated FAQ.
